blocks.text: Designed and shipped agentic LLM workflows that helped triage, research, and resolve customer support and data quality cases in a regulated healthcare environment. The system reduced manual handling, improved resolution speed, and maintained compliance guardrails through human review, evaluation criteria, and audit logging.
Problem

Support and operations teams manually researched repetitive data quality and customer issue cases. Case context was fragmented across systems, slowing investigation and resolution. Teams needed automation, but regulated healthcare workflows required human review, quality thresholds, and auditability.
Support and operations teams manually researched repetitive cases.
Case context was fragmented across systems.
Resolution quality varied by case complexity and operator experience.
High-risk healthcare workflows required reviewable and traceable AI assistance.
Manual handling increased operational cost and slowed customer response times.
My Role
As Product Manager, I owned product strategy, workflow design, evaluation criteria, risk guardrails, and launch execution.

I partnered with Engineering, Security, Customer Success, Support, and clinical operations to define which case types were safe to automate, where human review was required, and how quality would be measured before broader rollout.
Solution
Built AI-assisted workflows that retrieved relevant context, generated draft resolutions, routed edge cases to humans, and logged automated actions for traceability.

items.title: Case triage and classification
items.description: Automated categorization of incoming cases by type, risk level, and available context.

items.title: Context retrieval
items.description: Pulled relevant data from internal knowledge and support systems to inform each resolution.

items.title: LLM-generated recommendations
items.description: AI-drafted resolution suggestions and recommended next actions surfaced per case.

items.title: Human-in-the-loop review
items.description: Mandatory review checkpoints for ambiguous, sensitive, or low-confidence cases.

items.title: Escalation routing
items.description: Automatic routing for cases below confidence thresholds or missing required context.

items.title: Audit logging
items.description: Full traceability of automated recommendations and actions for compliance review.

items.title: Evaluation criteria
items.description: Quality, relevance, and compliance thresholds defined before rollout.

blocks.text: Execution and Stakeholder Management
Prioritized repetitive, high-volume case types with clear resolution patterns before expanding to ambiguous workflows.
Defined automation eligibility rules based on case risk, available context, and confidence thresholds.
Built human-in-the-loop checkpoints before scaling to reduce compliance and quality risk.
Partnered with Engineering to design workflows for triage, retrieval, recommendation generation, and escalation routing.
Partnered with Security and Compliance to ensure AI-assisted actions remained traceable and reviewable.
Used Support and Customer Success feedback to refine prompts, routing logic, and exception handling.
Key Product Decisions / Tradeoffs

blocks.tradeoff: Automated low-risk, repetitive cases first while routing ambiguous or sensitive cases to human review.
blocks.outcome: Reduced manual effort without exposing regulated workflows to unreviewed AI output.

blocks.tradeoff: Optimized for resolution speed only after defining quality thresholds and review checkpoints.
blocks.outcome: Teams trusted the system enough to use it in production operations.

blocks.tradeoff: Used LLMs to assist triage and resolution while keeping actions logged and reviewable.
blocks.outcome: Maintained traceability required for healthcare compliance.

blocks.tradeoff: Focused the MVP on case types with strong resolution patterns and available context.
blocks.outcome: Created a reliable foundation before expanding to more complex workflows.

blocks.text: Results & Impact
Resolved 2,000+ support and data quality cases through AI-assisted workflows.
Reduced manual effort by approximately $220K annually.
Improved resolution speed by reducing repetitive case research and manual triage.
Maintained healthcare compliance traceability through audit logging.
Created a reusable foundation for future AI-assisted operations use cases.
Reduced operational burden while preserving human oversight for high-risk cases.
Metrics Tracked
Number of AI-assisted cases resolved
Manual effort reduced
Annual cost savings
Human review / escalation rate
AI recommendation acceptance rate
Time to resolution
Audit traceability for automated actions
Case types eligible for automation
Key Learnings
AI workflow automation works best when focused on repetitive, high-volume workflows with clear resolution patterns.
In regulated environments, the product challenge is not just automation — it is designing the right balance of autonomy, human review, and traceability.
Human-in-the-loop systems build trust because teams can review, override, and improve AI-generated recommendations.
Evaluation criteria and escalation paths are essential before scaling LLM workflows into production operations.
